free_text: less accurate results for La-139, Gd-158 and Ho-165
.The thermal cross sections and ASSUM data are taken
from Goldman et al., Int.Report FFX-105,
Resonance activation integrals of some lanthanide
JEEP II heavy water reactor at Kjeller.
The thermal neutron flux was about
1.5 x 10**13 n/cm2/sec, and the cadmium ratio of
2.5 keV (FWHM) at 1331 keV peak of Co-60.
(RI) was calculated from the well known relation,
where sigma0 and CR are the thermal cross section and
.For the difference in thermal and epithermal flux
when a Cd-filter is used or not.
Goldman et al. for the cross-section-values (see ASSUM)
plus standard deviation for the cadmium ratios.
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
Corrections according last EXFOR rules and Dict.
Changes in SF4 of REACTION code in Subents 5,6, 12.
MONIT and MONIT-ERR added to COMMON section.
HALF-LIFE -> DECAY-DATA; HL deleted from DATA section
(value moved to DECAY-DATA) in Subents 5, 6, 12. Ref.
COMMENT deleted (same info in REL-REF). MONITOR was
added in Subents 2-12. ASSUM added to Subent 001.
Resonance integral for the building of the ground state
from DATA section (value moved to DECAY-DATA).
Resonance integral for the building of the metastable
from DATA section (value moved to DECAY-DATA).
.Value for the building of the ground state and
for part of the metastable state  (internal
transition) which decays to the ground state.
.Value for the building of the ground state and
for part of the metastable state  (internal
transition) which decays to the ground state.
.Value for the building of the ground and metastable
from DATA section (value moved to DECAY-DATA).
